creator: jdc@koitsu.org
text:
procmail's formail utility contains a heap overflow which can cause a denial-of-service if given certain malformed mail.

Details:

https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2017-16844
https://bugs.debian.org/cgi-bin/bugreport.cgi?bug=876511
https://www.debian.org/security/2017/dsa-4041
https://usn.ubuntu.com/usn/usn-3483-1/

Patch for 3.22, which I'll also attach, but be aware FreeBSD uses procmail 3.21 and I'm unsure if the patch is compatible:

https://bugs.debian.org/cgi-bin/bugreport.cgi?bug=876511#10

If upgrading to procmail 3.22 is thus required, this is the location for the code (the official procmail website has been down for almost 4 months now): http://www.ring.gr.jp/archives/net/mail/procmail/procmail-3.22.tar.gz
